MY PLAYGROUND💣💥☠️🦾 on Instagram: "The benefits of landmine workouts..✍️👇🏾

The landmine is a great tool for these times when supplementary training comes into play. Using a landmine reduces the presence of axial/direct loading on the joints, and it can change the position of movement and the range of motion by adjusting how the body and bar are positioned.
.
.

#movebetter #landmineworkout #makeithappen #stayfitandnatural #fitinspo #landminefitness #traindifferent #bodystrength #workout #fitfam"

DJerwpVxUR1.og_title:
Joe McMahan on Instagram: "Start training your stride like you actually want to get faster.
 Not just survive the miles—dominate them.

You don’t build a powerful, resilient stride by jogging and hoping for the best.
 You build it with progressive overload, intentional movement, and strength in the right places:

✅ Hips ✅ Quads ✅ Glutes ✅ Calves ✅ Core

Here’s exercises I keep in rotation to build stride strength and stay injury-resistant while training for endurance goals:

– 3x5 ATG Split Squats (knee & hip strength + mobility) – 3x10 Kettlebell Hip Marches (hip flexor + core control) – 3x8 Landmine Twists (rotational core power) – 3x6 Hip Thrusts (glute drive = power and posture) – 3x12 Single-Leg Calf Raises (ankle stiffness + toe-off) – 3x6 Poliquin Step Ups (knee bulletproofing) – 3x6 Goblet Squats (total-leg strength + posture)

Runners need to life too— but, lift like an athlete, not a body builder.
 Save this session and start moving better—every mile will feel different.

#strengthforrunners #marathontraining #runstrong #hybridathlete #injuryprevention #runningform #gymforrunners #runnersofinstagram #stridepower #posteriorchain #endurancefitness #trainingtips #athletedevelopment"

DKfWGIStm6H.og_title:
Valentina Schaub on Instagram: "Gym work for runners — it’s about boosting performance, preventing injury, and moving like an athlete 🔥🏃🏼‍♀️

7 key focus areas:
1️⃣ Hops
- quick, reactive, low-amplitude movements to improve stiffness and speed
2️⃣ Jumps
- explosive power for stronger strides and takeoff
3️⃣ Mobility
- hips, ankles, thoracic spine to move freely and efficiently
4️⃣ Back Chain Strength
- glutes, hamstrings, calves: the engine room for runners
5️⃣ Single Leg Exercises
- control, alignment, and strength (running is single-leg!)
6️⃣ Foot & Ankle Work
- barefoot drills, balance, strength exercises for injury resistance
7️⃣ Core Control
- build posture

These are the 7 things I never skip in the gym as an athlete — not just to run faster, but to run longer, stronger, and injury-free.🤝🏼🏃🏼‍♀️🔥

.
 #fitness #gym #workout #fitnessmotivation #fit #motivation #bikini #training #health #travel #healthylifestyle #lifestyle #love #gymlife #gymmotivation #sport #summer #healthy #healthyfood #muscle #running #fitnessmodel #exercise #food #fitnessjourney #fashion #happy #girl #model #style"

DN3fc4G4ptW.og_title:
Meg Takacs on Instagram: "5 reasons why tight hips can actually be weak hips:
‌
1. Protective tension: when your hips lack strength, your body creates “tightness” to protect unstable joints.
2. Overworked muscles: weak glutes, hip flexors and surrounding muscles pick up the slack for weak hips and tighten up.
3. Instability: your muscles lock up more when your hips can’t stabilize
4. Limited range of motion: weakness reduces control and restricts full movement capacity
5. Knees and low back work harder which creates more tension in the hips
‌
Download my app for guided workouts!
Movement & Miles: first month is FREE!"

DOiaWDuiO12.og_title:
Sportsmenacademy on Instagram: "⚽️ 11 Football/Soccer Exercises To Develop Explosiveness, Movement, and Agility:

✅ Follow @sportsmenacademy for more great content 🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥

This sport requires quick, efficient movement, while also having to be fast, and explosive while moving in the lateral/linear plane. All of these exercises target exactly that. 

Ex 1: Develops the body’s ability to & produce force efficiently. Love this for explosive training

Ex 2: Develops concentric explosive power, teaching the body to be explosive from the ground up- developing force production 

Ex 3,5,7,10,11: These all teach the body to be explosive in the LATERAL plane, developing the body’s ability to change direction efficiently. 

Ex 4,6: Develops single leg explosiveness power, eliminating muscle imbalances 

Ex 8: Develops ankle strength + lateral movement. Love prepping the body with this movement prior to a later day

Ex 9: Is great for building single leg strength within the knees/hips. Great for eliminating muscle imbalances, and laying a solid foundation to prevent injury, and have great movement/speed
______________________________________
This Summer, Starting June 2nd I will be taking 20 people to train online with me! Here’s The Details👇🏾

•8 Week 1 on 1 Online Training With Me 
-Weekly Checkins (Video Call)
-Daily Exercise Critique
-Full 8 Week Workout Program
-Video Examples of Every Exercise. 
-24/7 Access To Me

DM me if you’d like to work💪🏾, serious inquiries please. 

🎥 @adonis.harrison

DOoILgMCI5A.og_title:
Radoslav Georgiev | P-Rehab, Exercise, Movement on Instagram: "Core Stability Is Your Shield 🛡️

Core training isn’t just about crunches. Your core is a system of muscles that stabilizes the body during every movement.

👉 A strong core means:

Lower risk of injuries 🛑

More strength in every exercise 🏋️

Better posture and body control

✅ Whether you’re lifting in the gym or just want to move without pain—working on core stability is non-negotiable.

🚨 Disclaimer: Always consult a medical professional before attempting any exercises or techniques shown on this channel.
🌐 Learn more about our services & products: www.omniprehab.com

#corestability #injuryprevention #functionalstrength #biomechanics #rehabcoach #sportsrehab #physiotherapyexercises #gymperformance #movementcoach #spinalhealth"

DT6JGR1iPuf.og_title:
Sportsmenacademy on Instagram: "#glutes #leg 

‼️Why do I use resistance bands so much?
‼️Why are these small bands so important?

Many people think a resistance band is just a simple tool, but the truth is, in professional training, it plays a golden role:

1. Activating muscles like flipping a switch:
Before jumping into squats, leg presses, or any heavy movements, your body needs to be fully warmed up and ready.
The band helps immediately activate the glutes and quads — it’s like turning on the engine of your muscles!

2. Precisely targeting hidden muscles:
Some muscles — like the gluteus medius or the vastus medialis — often stay under-activated during compound exercises.
The band pulls them into action!
This leads to better muscular balance and cleaner movement patterns.

3. Improving form and control:
Even if you’ve been training for years, your knees might cave in during squats, or your glutes might not stay properly engaged.
The band helps correct these mistakes, keeping your movements clean, strong, and professional.

4. Reducing injury risk:
Inactive or weak muscles are a major cause of injuries!
By using a band, you wake up these muscles, giving extra support to your knees, hips, and lower back — and helping prevent injuries.

🎥 @workout_athletics 
#legdayworkout # #miniloop homeworkout bandworkout performance athletic functionaltrainer aminhashemi 

Do you use mini loop bands?👇"

DTC9kbVCJEB.og_title:
Sportsmenacademy on Instagram: "HIP MOBILITY LOVE ❤️ 
-
 Taking care of your hips is a must 🫶🏼. Hip mobility is essential to proper full functioning of the hip joint. Mobile hips allow athletes to become more powerful and perform athletic movements more efficiently. For individuals who sit a lot greater hip mobility will aid in helping with issues such as back pain and knee pain that can be caused from over compensation. The hips are one of the most common sites of poor mobility, therefore many of us will experience a dysfunction and loss of strength in that area. It is very important to find a healthy balance between mobility and strength! 
-
THE WORK 🔨:

Perform 45 sec-1 Min per movement 1-2X 

Hope this helps 

As always,

Peace and Movement 🤞🏽

🎥 @protein.papi_
#mobilitywod #mobilitywork #hipmobility #hipmobilitywork #mobilityexercises torontotrainer torontopersonaltrainer"

DUK9EhsiFza.og_title:
Sportsmenacademy on Instagram: "Slide 1 - Knee extension: If you suffer a knee injury or have just undergone knee surgery, your terminal knee extension will likely be limited. This means that you will not be able to lock your knee in place. The goal of knee extension exercises in this scenario is to essentially help you to slowly improve, or extend the end range of motion in your knees.
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
Slide 2 - Knee mobility: The knee is usually trained in sagittal plane, but rarely if ever in a transverse plane. Start training your knee in all degrees 
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
Slide 3 - Knee flexion/quad stretch: The key to improve knee flexion after surgery is gently pushing into that uncomfortable range and trying to progressively increase the amount of time you spend there with each successive day. In this drill I am using a prone band quad stretch PNF of 5s:5s. Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation (PNF) is a more advanced form of flexibility training. PNF involves both stretching and contracting (activation) of the muscle group being targeted in order to achieve maximum static flexibility.
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
Slide 4 - Isometrics: Isometric exercise is the technique of engaging your muscles without movement. Isometrics strengthens your connective tissues. Tendons and ligaments are the parts of your body that hold everything together. The stronger your connective tissue is, the stronger you will be. In addition, the stronger your tendons and ligaments are the less likely you are to be injured.
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
✅ Save this post
🤳🏽 Send it to a friend or tag him/her
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
🎥 @dreamchaserr.__ 
⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀
